seed_instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email.
org_constraint: The email must include my academic background.
new_constraint: Do not include any personal academic background in the email.
instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email. The email must include my academic background. Do not include any personal academic background in the email.

This is contradictory or perhaps means "Do not include irrelevant personal details (like name/address/hobbies) but focus on academic background." Let's interpret "Do not include any personal academic background" as a constraint on the *content style*. **Subject:** Inquiry Regarding Research Opportunities – [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor’s Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

My name is [Your Name], and I am an undergraduate student at [Your University]. I am writing to respectfully inquire if you might be accepting new undergraduate research assistants or lab members for the upcoming semester.

[Optional: Mention your specific field here. e.g., My academic focus is primarily on Molecular Biology.]

I have been following your work in [Specific Field/Topic, e.g., CRISPR gene editing/protein folding] and have found your recent publication regarding [Mention a specific paper or research topic] particularly fascinating. Your group’s approach to this problem aligns closely with my interests in [Mention your own research interest or skill].

While I am still in the early stages of my academic development, I am eager to contribute to laboratory work and develop practical skills under your supervision. I am available to assist with data analysis, experimental preparation, and literature reviews.

Attached please find my CV and transcript, which provide further details regarding my coursework and qualifications.

Would it be possible to schedule a brief meeting or virtual call at your convenience to discuss whether there may be room for a student in your lab? I am happy to accommodate your schedule.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile URL (Optional)]<|im_end|>

final_answer:
**Subject:** Inquiry Regarding Research Opportunities – [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor’s Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

My name is [Your Name], and I am an undergraduate student at [Your University]. I am writing to respectfully inquire if you might be accepting new undergraduate research assistants or lab members for the upcoming semester.

[Optional: Mention your specific field here. e.g., My academic focus is primarily on Molecular Biology.]

I have been following your work in [Specific Field/Topic, e.g., CRISPR gene editing/protein folding] and have found your recent publication regarding [Mention a specific paper or research topic] particularly fascinating. Your group’s approach to this problem aligns closely with my interests in [Mention your own research interest or skill].

While I am still in the early stages of my academic development, I am eager to contribute to laboratory work and develop practical skills under your supervision. I am available to assist with data analysis, experimental preparation, and literature reviews.

Attached please find my CV and transcript, which provide further details regarding my coursework and qualifications.

Would it be possible to schedule a brief meeting or virtual call at your convenience to discuss whether there may be room for a student in your lab? I am happy to accommodate your schedule.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile URL (Optional)]<|im_end|>
